---
title: "About"
---

## About LightSPAN

Light exposure is vital for human health and wellbeing. Research shows that receiving the right light at the right time supports vision, sleep, cognitive well-being and overall functioning.

LightSPAN is a multidisciplinary research project that adopts a **lifespan-oriented approach**, targeting two key populations in Singapore: **school-aged children and older adults**. The project investigates the efficacy of optimising daily light exposure to improve health outcomes across the life course, with a focus on **vision, sleep and cognitive well-being**.

By addressing light as a **modifiable environmental factor**, LightSPAN aims to support longer lives spent in good health.

---

## The LightUP App

At the core of LightSPAN is **LightUP**, an innovative and non-invasive behaviour change programme delivered through a mobile health (mHealth) smartphone application.

The LightUP app:

- Provides personalised and timely light exposure recommendations
- Uses onboard sensors to track and visualise real-time light exposure
- Delivers feedback-driven prompts to support sustainable, light-friendly behaviours in everyday life

The goal is to make healthier light exposure practices **accessible and sustainable in daily routines**.

---

## School-aged Children

Singapore is often referred to as the **myopia capital of the world**, due to the high prevalence of myopia among school-aged children.

In this population, LightSPAN aims to:

- Prevent or delay the onset of myopia
- Reduce its long-term public health impact
- Support healthy visual development during childhood

The programme actively involves **parents** in supporting their children’s daily light habits, helping to ensure adequate healthy daylight exposure as part of everyday routines to promote optimal development and cognitive functioning.

---

## Older Adults

For older adults, LightSPAN focuses on supporting **healthy ageing** through optimised light exposure.

The project investigates how improved light environments can support:

- Regulation of the biological clock
- Better sleep quality
- Improved mood
- Cognitive and physical function
- Overall wellbeing

This work is particularly relevant given **Singapore’s rapidly ageing population**. Through accessible digital tools and light-aware daily routines, LightSPAN seeks to help preserve functional ability and quality of life in later life.

---

## Partnerships

The LightSPAN project is led by **TUMCREATE** in collaboration with:

- National University of Singapore (NUS)
- Lions Befrienders Singapore

The project also involves partnerships with key government agencies.

Together, these collaborations aim to generate **robust scientific evidence** and contribute to the development of **evidence-based guidelines and recommendations** that support light-optimised lifestyles and healthier communities across Singapore.

---

## Funding

The LightSPAN project is supported by funding from the **National Research Foundation (NRF), Singapore**.
